title: Spot the change
prompt: You have the config as it was reviewed (sshd_config.orig) and the config running now (sshd_config.live). They should be identical. diff FILE1 FILE2 prints only the lines that differ. Find what was slipped into the live config — the flag rides on the injected line.

files.contents:
Port 22
PermitRootLogin no
PasswordAuthentication yes
MaxAuthTries 4
AllowUsers ty deploy

Port 22
PermitRootLogin no
PasswordAuthentication yes
MaxAuthTries 4
AllowUsers ty deploy backdoor_{{FLAG}}

details.restate: Compare the two config files and find the one line that differs. The extra user on that line carries the flag — submit just the flag part.

io.returns: (only the changed line, shown twice: before < and after >)
io.because: diff stays silent about everything identical — its whole job is the delta

help.plain: Two nearly-identical files, one command that prints only where they disagree.
help.analogy: Spot-the-difference puzzles — except the computer circles it for you.
help.mnemonic: diff old new: < is what old said, > is what new says.
help.nudge: Reading both files line by line works at 5 lines. What command still works at 5,000?
